feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Michigan sees sharp rise in nitrous oxide use that lead to ER visits
entry.summary: Michiganders are inreasingly showing up in hospital ERs, calling 911 or the state poison center after using nitrous oxide. Here's why that matters.

entry.title: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo & Uganda
entry.summary: The Bundibugyo virus disease (BVD) outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo continues to evolve rapidly, with sustained transmission and increasing numbers of reported cases. As of 17 June, a cumulative of 896 confirmed cases, including 232 deaths, ha…

entry.title: Non-surgical procedure may help relieve knee pain
entry.summary: Osteoarthritis, the most common form of arthritis, causes inflammation, stiffness, reduced mobility and sensory nerve pain.

entry.title: Guest Column | Facing cancer, I now understand the hidden lesson in my father’s final days
entry.summary: As a Holocaust survivor, my father knew about adversity and survival. But I'm learning something else from the end of his life.

entry.title: Little-known tick bite disease leaves man nonverbal — what to know about the rising threat
entry.summary: According to friends and family, John Reagan, 66, is nonverbal after he was bitten by a tick while walking his dog.

entry.title: Ebola in DR Congo: Six-year-old 'doing well' after armed men took her from hospital
entry.summary: Health facilities have come under attack during the current outbreak as a result of misinformation and fear.

entry.title: Legionnaires’ disease outbreak in Philadelphia in 1976 was mysterious and deadly – 50 years later, scientists know the cause but outbreaks continue
entry.summary: The source was a newly discovered bacterium called Legionella pneumophila that can thrive in household pipes, whirlpools, cooling units and outdoor fountains.

entry.title: We Are Admittedly a Bit Startled by This Medical Case Report About Giving an Elderly Woman With Advanced Alzheimer's a Gigantic Dose of Psychedelic Mushrooms Just to See What Would Happen
entry.summary: A recent case report describes an octogenarian woman with Alzheimer's being given a shockingly high dose of psilocybin.

entry.title: Could the HPV vaccine eliminate cervical cancer deaths?
entry.summary: The HPV vaccine has reduced the risk of cervical cancer death before age 30 to effectively zero in the United Kingdom.

entry.title: The Medford pharmacy killings: A 15-year-old spotlight on Long Island's opioid crisis
entry.summary: Fifteen years after the quadruple homicides, progress on stemming the opioid crisis is mixed with memories of the killings and the innocents lost.

entry.title: RFK Jr.’s New Lyme Plan Is More Conspiracy Than Science
entry.summary: Chronic Lyme disease is a dubious medical diagnosis, but that isn't stopping RFK Jr. from boosting it.

entry.title: A Signal of Future Alzheimer's May Be Hidden in Your Eyes
entry.summary: With every year, more evidence is connecting our eyes to Alzheimer's disease.

entry.title: I’m an infectious disease doc — the way-too-common things people do that make me squirm
entry.summary: What’s worse than spreading a disease? Not even realizing when you’re doing it.

entry.title: Deep learning reveals antimicrobial peptides within prions
entry.summary: Deep learning reveals that prion-related proteins contain hidden ‘prionin’ peptides with antibacterial activity, including two candidates that reduced infections in mice.

entry.title: Pseudoscientific Cancer ‘Treatment’ Involves Gassing Naked People in Plastic Bags With Bleach
entry.summary: A London clinic owner has claimed he is treating people with stage 4 cancer by sealing them into a plastic bag while they're naked from the waist down and gassing them with chlorine dioxide.

entry.title: ‘Ghost fat’ is a surprising side effect of injectable weight-loss drugs
entry.summary: People experiencing "ghost fat" may instinctively continue behaviors they developed when they were larger.

entry.title: Thousands of baby seals died on two remote sub-Antarctic islands. Scientists now think they know why
entry.summary: A deadly strain of bird flu sweeping through remote islands near Antarctica has devastated the native wildlife population, killing an estimated 13,000 seal pups, as well as penguins and seabirds, researchers say.

entry.title: Thousands of baby seals died on two remote sub-Antarctic islands. Scientists now think they know why
entry.summary: A deadly strain of bird flu sweeping through remote islands near Antarctica has devastated the native wildlife population, killing an estimated 13,000 seal pups, as well as penguins and seabirds, researchers say.

entry.title: Ebola patients flee treatment centres in Congo for food as hunger crisis deepens
entry.summary: Over 150 escapes from Ebola treatment and isolation facilities have been documented since late May. Read more at straitstimes.com. Read more at straitstimes.com.

entry.title: Rabid stray cat bites 5 Florida residents and attacks puppy before dying under a home
entry.summary: A woman said the cat jumped into her lap, bit one of her fingers, and then charged at her legs until she drove it away with a baseball bat.

entry.title: Granddaughter Pays for Her Grandmother's Care — but Watching Her Ignore Doctor's Warnings Pushed Her to a Breaking Point
entry.summary: The best of the internet, sifted, and sorted.

entry.title: Student, 18, Who Suffered Severe Burns from a House Fire Recovers After World-First Experimental Treatment
entry.summary: Doctors called the results "absolutely remarkable"

entry.title: Local Army vet fighting for his life in ICU, daughter says Legionnaires’ disease is to blame
entry.summary: A longtime employee at the Pittsburgh VA Medical Center was hospitalized with Legionnaires’ disease, according to his daughter.

entry.title: The "dad bod," explained by science
entry.summary: The term is more than a cultural phenomenon.

entry.title: Deadly tick-borne disease that can cause brain damage spreading across the US
entry.summary: Authorities are sounding the alarm about a rare, but potentially deadly tick-borne disease as tick season ramps up across the U.S. Powassan virus is an extremely rare but serious illness spread through the bite of infected black-legged deer ticks.

entry.title: Joint-Pain Supplement Tied to Faster Alzheimer's Progression
entry.summary: Researchers say findings on glucosamine are preliminary and show association, not causation

entry.title: The omega-3 supplement you take may not be helping your brain
entry.summary: Taking an omega-3 fatty acid supplement did not improve memory, cognitive function or brain cell loss for many people, a surprising new study finds.

entry.title: Bizarre videos claiming sunburns ‘aren’t dangerous’ are flooding TikTok, study warns
entry.summary: The heat is on!

entry.title: 3 Everyday Habits with More Blood Pressure Benefits Than Daily Walks
entry.summary: A cardiologist and a dietitian share their expert tips for battling hypertension. Dietary changes, getting enough hydration and sleeping sufficiently can all combat high blood pressure.

entry.title: Ebola outbreak zone cases up almost 40 percent in a week
entry.summary: The Ebola outbreak in Congo that’s spread across parts of Africa has killed more than 200 people in its first month and is considered the worst known outbreak at this stage, the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) said Thursday. Ebo…

entry.title: Scientists Link 8 Common Food Additives to Heart Disease Risk
entry.summary: Certain food preservatives might silently be raising our blood pressure and heart disease risk, recent research has found.

entry.title: What is The Healthiest Type of Olive Oil? The No. 1 Pick with the Most Benefits
entry.summary: Olive oil comes in many varieties. Which type is healthiest? Experts reveal the olive oil with the most heart-healthy fats and anti-inflammatory antioxidants.

entry.title: Ticks are spreading Lyme disease across America, but we can beat them. Here’s how.
entry.summary: A Lyme disease vaccine, alpha-gal-treatments, and good habits to get rid of ticks can help us stop these diseases caused by deer and dog tick bites.

entry.title: Rare Lyme disease-causing strain spreads to new state: What to know
entry.summary: A rare pathogen known to cause Lyme disease has been detected in a new state, far from where it has typically been found.

entry.title: On TikTok, sunscreen misinformation goes viral
entry.summary: Sunscreen videos on TikTok often go viral, but the most popular may spread misinformation, a PLOS Digital Health study found. University of Alberta study.

entry.title: Mom Thought She Had a Boil. Then She Vomited 'Black Tar', Ended Up in a Coma from Flesh-Eating Disease
entry.summary: Caroline Fonjock needed emergency surgery after she developed an aggressive case of the flesh-eating disease that left her leg looking 'like roadkill'

entry.title: A Popular Joint Pain Supplement May Actually Speed Up Dementia Progression
entry.summary: New research suggests that glucosamine may accelerate cognitive decline in people with symptoms. Here's what to know.

entry.title: Shingles vaccine(s) and dementia: the only medical intervention to work faster than Tums
entry.summary: Do you believe in magic?

entry.title: The supplement industry's dirty secret: Why many supplements fail to deliver results
entry.summary: Despite the massive growth of the supplement industry in recent years, many people still struggle to feel noticeable results from the products they take every day.

entry.title: Cancer patients are choosing ivermectin over chemo, and doctors are concerned
entry.summary: Ivermectin, the anti-parasitic medication most commonly used as a dewormer in animals, is back in the limelight. This time it has nothing to do with COVID-19.

entry.title: Your pelvic floor weakens as you get older. Here’s how to keep it strong for life.
entry.summary: Weakness in this part of the body becomes increasingly common with age, causing urinary incontinence and other symptoms. But it’s also treatable.

entry.title: Scientists Discover Surprising Link Between Birth Control Pills and Emotional Eating
entry.summary: Women actively taking a combined contraceptive pill seem more likely to emotionally eat, a new study has found.

entry.title: 42 babies die in Australia and cases of devastating STI spike in US — doc warns he’s ‘never been more concerned’
entry.summary: Left untreated, it can lead to miscarriage, premature birth, skeletal abnormalities, neurological problems, developmental delays, stillbirth or infant death shortly after birth.

entry.title: People possibly exposed to rabies after picking up injured bat in Evergreen
entry.summary: Several people may have been exposed to rabies after reports of people picking up and touching an injured bat in Evergreen.

entry.title: Hunter-gatherers in Siberia died of a plague outbreak 5,500 years ago
entry.summary: We can't blame the Neolithic Transition for the plague anymore.

entry.title: The Number-1 Sign of Colon Cancer Doctors Say You Shouldn’t Ignore
entry.summary: Gastroenterologists reveal the number-one sign of colon cancer. Plus, other symptoms to watch for and why the illness can be difficult to diagnose.

entry.title: The head of the family is 17. Money is tight. The roof leaks. How did this happen?
entry.summary: Three brothers say their mother and father died after losing access to their HIV medications. Now the boys are figuring out how to navigate life.

entry.title: South African scientists make breakthrough in decoding cancer’s most effective survival strategy
entry.summary: The goal is to develop treatments that can strip away the sugar shield cancer uses to hide from the immune system.

entry.title: AI helped diagnose 18 children whose rare diseases had stumped doctors
entry.summary: Researchers from Boston Children’s Hospital identified 18 diagnoses for children with rare diseases using OpenAI’s o3 model.

entry.title: 7 unexpected takeaways from the newest research on cannabis and brain effects
entry.summary: Whether it’s used in adolescence, midlife or older age may make a big difference.
